a: GlobalG.A.P. is a leading global farm assurance program that translates consumer requirements into Good Agricultural Practice (G.A.P.) into a widely adopted certification standard.

q: How does GS1 Digital Link enhance traceability?
a: GS1 Digital Link enables traditional product identifiers, like GTINs, to become web-enabled, allowing access to a wealth of product information, including sustainability data and certification status, via a simple scan.
A GGN (GlobalG.A.P. Number) is a unique 13-digit identification number assigned to every producer registered in the GlobalG.A.P. certification system, used for verifying certification status.

q: How does GlobalG.A.P. align with the EU Farm to Fork Strategy?
a: GlobalG.A.P. standards, particularly IFA v6, align with the EU Farm to Fork Strategy by promoting sustainable farming practices, reducing pesticide use, and enhancing environmental protection.

q: Why is GlobalG.A.P. important for South African agriculture?
a: GlobalG.A.P. certification is crucial for South African agricultural exporters to meet international market requirements, particularly in the EU and UK, ensuring food safety, sustainability, and market access.
